place: Fayetteville village, New York, New York

facts.text: Fayetteville is named after the Marquis de Lafayette, a hero of both France and the United States.
First settlers arrived in Fayetteville in 1792, making it one of the earliest communities in Onondaga County.
Fayetteville was officially incorporated in 1844, after more than five decades of growth and development.
The Charles Estabrook Mansion, Genesee Street Hill‑Limestone Plaza Historic District, and Levi Snell House are listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
The Fayetteville Free Library earned a 5‑star rating from Library Journal in 2009 and now hosts a makerspace with 3D printers and a CNC router.
A former railroad right‑of‑way is being repurposed for community use, preserving Fayetteville’s transportation heritage.
